sections.paragraph:
ἐπικαλύπτω fut. ἐπικαλύψω; 1 aor. ἐπικάλυψα LXX. Pass.: 1 aor. ἐπεκαλύφθην; pf. ptc. ἐπικεκαλυμμένος 2 Km 5:30 (s. prec. entry; Hes. et al.; Just., D. 68, 6 and 130, 1 ἐπικεκαλυμμένως) ‘to hide from view by covering’, then by fig. extension as synonym for ‘forgive’ cover up ὧν ἐπεκαλύφθησαν αἱ ἁμαρτίαι whose sins are put out of sight Ro 4:7; 1 Cl 50:6 (both Ps 31:1).

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

prec. prec. = preceding

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 373
